description:
## Problem Statement

Beadsmith can now show the three-pane Issue Explorer chrome and list real Issues from the Current Workspace, but selecting an Issue still leaves the right pane as a placeholder. As a developer browsing Beadwork work, I need to click an Issue and read its full context without leaving Beadsmith or falling back to the `bw` CLI.

The Issue Detail must make the selected Issue readable without turning Beadsmith into a second issue tracker. Beadwork remains the source of truth; Beadsmith should present the Issue data it already loads from `bw list --all --json` through a richer detail view.

## Solution

When the user clicks an Issue in the Issue List, Beadsmith selects that Issue and renders an Issue Detail in the right pane. The pane stays empty until the user selects an Issue. The selected row remains highlighted, and clicking the already-selected row does not deselect it.

The Issue Detail shows the selected Issue as a readable view: de-emphasized ID and title, concise primary metadata, labels, Markdown description, dependency context, other metadata, and read-only Markdown comments when present. Empty optional fields stay hidden except where an explicit empty state clarifies meaning, such as an empty description or empty dependency groups. Closed Issues should include close reason in Other metadata when Beadwork provides it.

The existing list data should become the full Issue contract. `bw list --all --json` already includes description and comments, so Beadsmith should not add a separate `bw show` call for this slice. The existing `IssueSummary` naming should be replaced with `Issue`, and list/detail presentations should be modeled as view-specific projections of that Issue.

## User Stories

1. As a Beadsmith user, I want to click an Issue in the Issue List, so that I can read its details in the right pane.
2. As a Beadsmith user, I want the detail pane to stay empty until I select an Issue, so that the app does not assume what I want to inspect.
3. As a Beadsmith user, I want the selected Issue row to be visually highlighted, so that I know which Issue the right pane is showing.
4. As a Beadsmith user, I want clicking the selected Issue again to keep it selected, so that accidental repeat clicks do not clear context.
5. As a Beadsmith user, I want the Issue ID to appear beside the title in a de-emphasized style, so that I can identify the Issue without the ID overpowering the title.
6. As a Beadsmith user, I want to see status, priority, and type near the title, so that I can quickly understand the Issue's current workflow state.
7. As a Beadsmith user, I want priority displayed as `P#`, so that it matches compact developer-tool conventions.
8. As a Beadsmith user, I want labels displayed as compact chips when present, so that I can scan categorization without reading every field.
9. As a Beadsmith user, I want empty labels hidden, so that the detail pane stays concise.
10. As a Beadsmith user, I want the description rendered as Markdown, so that formatted Beadwork descriptions are readable.
11. As a Beadsmith user, I want GitHub-flavored Markdown features to render, so that common tables, task lists, strikethrough, and autolinks work as expected.
12. As a Beadsmith user, I want raw HTML in Markdown to remain disabled, so that Issue content cannot inject arbitrary HTML into the app.
13. As a Beadsmith user, I want external HTTP(S) Markdown links to be clickable, so that I can follow web references from Issue descriptions and comments without navigating the app webview.
14. As a Beadsmith user, I want an empty description to show a clear icon-and-text empty state with minimum height, so that a blank description is intentional rather than looking broken.
15. As a Beadsmith user, I want to see which Issue IDs block the selected Issue, so that I understand what must happen before it can proceed.
16. As a Beadsmith user, I want to see which Issue IDs the selected Issue blocks, so that I understand what completing it would unblock.
17. As a Beadsmith user, I want dependency groups to show explicit empty states such as “No blockers,” so that an absence of dependencies is clear.
18. As a Beadsmith user, I want dependencies shown as raw Issue IDs for now, so that the feature avoids extra resolution behavior.
19. As a Beadsmith user, I want dependency context in one compact Dependencies card, so that both directions are easy to scan together.
20. As a Beadsmith user, I want assignee shown only when present, so that optional metadata does not create clutter.
21. As a Beadsmith user, I want created and updated values shown as raw strings for now, so that the UI reflects Beadwork data without adding date-formatting rules.
22. As a Beadsmith user, I want due, deferred-until, closed-at, close reason, parent, and other optional metadata shown only when present, so that the metadata section remains concise.
23. As a Beadsmith user, I want comments displayed read-only when they exist, so that I can read discussion without switching tools.
24. As a Beadsmith user, I want comments rendered as Markdown, so that links, code, and lists in comments are readable.
25. As a Beadsmith user, I want comment timestamps shown as raw strings, so that I can see when each comment was made.
26. As a Beadsmith user, I want comment author shown when present, so that attributed comments retain their context.
27. As a Beadsmith user, I want Issues with no comments to omit the comments section or show no noisy placeholder, so that the pane focuses on available content.
28. As a Beadsmith user, I want Issue data normalized so missing comments become an empty array, so that the app handles Beadwork's omitted fields consistently.
29. As a Beadsmith user, I want Issue data normalized so missing descriptions become an empty string, so that empty descriptions render predictably.
30. As a Beadsmith user, I want the Issue List to continue showing the same compact row information after the data type rename, so that the existing browsing experience is preserved.
31. As a Beadsmith user, I want selecting Issues to feel instantaneous, so that browsing remains fast and desktop-like.
32. As a keyboard user, I want issue rows implemented as accessible clickable controls, so that keyboard support can be added without reworking semantics later.
33. As a maintainer, I want `IssueSummary` renamed to `Issue`, so that the code matches the domain model and does not imply the data is only a summary.
34. As a maintainer, I want list and detail rendering to use separate view models, so that each UI surface can evolve without bloating the domain contract.
35. As a maintainer, I want the existing Issue List tests preserved through the rename, so that the compact list projection remains covered.
36. As a maintainer, I want fast component tests around the detail pane and Markdown rendering, so that regressions are caught without launching the desktop app.
37. As a maintainer, I want WebDriver coverage extended for selecting an Issue and seeing detail content, so that the real Tauri/TauRPC/Effect/React path remains proven.
38. As an agent implementing this feature, I want the acceptance behavior expressed in domain terms, so that the implementation does not introduce a second issue model.

## Implementation Decisions

- Use **Issue** as the canonical frontend/native data contract for Beadwork issue data loaded from the Current Workspace.
- Rename the existing summary-oriented RPC and frontend naming from `IssueSummary` / `list_issue_summaries` to `Issue` / `list_issues`.
- Do not add a new detail RPC or call `bw show` for this slice. The app should use the full Issue records already returned by `bw list --all --json`.
- Preserve Beadwork as the source of truth. Beadsmith may normalize and present Issue data, but it must not create a separate durable Issue model.
- Normalize `description` to an always-present string. Missing or null raw descriptions become `""`.
- Normalize `comments` to an always-present array. Missing, null, or omitted raw comments become `[]`.
- Normalize comment `author` to an always-present string. Missing author becomes `""` and is hidden by the view when empty.
- Add `closeReason` to the normalized Issue contract. Missing or null raw close reasons become `""` and are hidden by the view when empty.
- Keep the existing list-loading behavior and failure model: loading, success with Issues, empty with workspace path, and failure with a typed error.
- Store only the selected Issue ID in React state. Derive the selected Issue from the loaded Issue collection.
- If the selected Issue disappears after a future reload, clear the selection and show the normal empty state.
- Clicking an Issue selects it. Clicking the already-selected Issue does not deselect it.
- Keep selection local to React state for this slice. Do not add URL, route, or history state.
- The detail pane stays empty until the user selects an Issue.
- Issue rows should become accessible clickable controls while avoiding broader keyboard navigation behavior in this slice.
- Add an Issue Detail view model as a frontend projection only. Do not add an `IssueDetail` RPC/domain type.
- Keep the compact Issue List projection as a separate view tailored to scanning rows.
- Add only the new component files needed for this slice rather than broadly decomposing the existing app.
- Add an `IssueDetailPane` component that receives `Issue | null` and owns both empty and selected rendering.
- Add a shared `MarkdownContent` component for Markdown used by both descriptions and comments.
- Place the shared Markdown renderer in generic component infrastructure rather than issue-specific code.
- Render Markdown with `react-markdown` and GitHub-flavored Markdown support via `remark-gfm`.
- Disable raw HTML rendering in Markdown.
- Make only external HTTP(S) Markdown links clickable and safe for a Tauri app. `http:` and `https:` links should open outside the app rather than navigating the webview. Relative links, Issue IDs, file paths, anchors, and non-HTTP schemes are not navigation features in this slice.
- Style Markdown paragraphs, headings, lists, inline code, and code blocks to match the existing dark developer-tool design.
- Do not add code-block copy buttons in this slice.
- Do not add a breadcrumb in this slice.
- Do not add a sticky or blurred scrolling header in this slice.
- Detail layout order is fixed for this slice: de-emphasized ID plus title; status / priority / type; labels; description; dependencies; other metadata; comments.
- Labels appear as compact chips below primary metadata and before the description, only when present.
- Dependencies appear in one compact card with `Blocked by` and `Blocking` rows.
- Dependency rows display raw Issue IDs only. Do not resolve titles/statuses and do not add dependency navigation in this slice.
- Other metadata includes optional assignee when present and raw date/string fields such as created, updated, due, defer-until, closed-at, close reason, and parent according to availability.
- Dates and timestamps are displayed as raw strings for now.
- Comments are read-only. Creating, editing, deleting, or replying to comments is out of scope.
- Comments render as Markdown with raw HTML disabled and links clickable.
- Comment metadata shows author when present and timestamp.

## Testing Decisions

- Tests should assert external behavior and user-visible outcomes rather than implementation details. A good test verifies what the user sees or what a public seam returns, not internal component structure.
- Preserve existing lower-level seams through the rename, and add the new primary fast UI seam at the component level. Prefer broad behavior coverage over duplicating the same assertion across every layer.
- Preserve and rename/refactor the existing Issue List unit tests. They remain relevant because the compact list projection still exists after `IssueSummary` becomes `Issue`.
- Keep adapter/RPC tests that prove `bw list --all --json` is invoked and normalized correctly. Extend them to cover `description`, `comments`, comment `author`, and `closeReason` normalization.
- Keep Effect service tests at the list-loading seam. Update names and transport methods to `Issue` / `listIssues`, while preserving loading, success, empty, and failure behavior.
- Add an Issue Detail view-model test seam for empty descriptions, dependency labels/empty states, optional metadata, labels, and comments.
- Add component tests using Vitest with React Testing Library, jest-dom, and jsdom. This provides the fast reliable feedback loop required for the detail UI. Configure Vitest to run component tests in a jsdom environment and load jest-dom matchers through a test setup file.
- Component tests should cover the empty detail state, selected Issue title/metadata/labels, Markdown description rendering, empty description state, dependency empty/non-empty states, and read-only comments with author/timestamp and Markdown.
- Component tests should cover the shared Markdown renderer's user-visible behavior, including Markdown text/code/link rendering and no raw HTML execution/rendering behavior. Use dependency injection for link opening: `MarkdownContent` should accept an overridable HTTP-link opener so tests can pass a test double instead of invoking Tauri. The production default can call the Tauri opener.
- Extend the existing WebDriver Issue List suite rather than creating a separate suite. The current suite already launches the real built desktop binary against a disposable Beadwork workspace.
- Keep project docs up to date while renaming and extending this slice. In particular, update WebDriver/E2E documentation and any references to `IssueSummary` or `list_issue_summaries` so they match the new `Issue` / `list_issues` language.
- Extend the E2E fixture to include a description and comments suitable for detail rendering assertions.
- Extend the E2E success scenario to click an Issue row and verify detail content renders through the full Rust adapter → TauRPC → Effect → React path.
- The E2E test should remain focused on the real user path and avoid over-asserting visual implementation details.
- Existing empty-list E2E behavior should continue to pass.

## Out of Scope

- No new `bw show` call or detail-specific RPC.
- No Issue editing.
- No comment creation, editing, deletion, or reply behavior.
- No dependency navigation.
- No dependency title/status resolution.
- No search/filter implementation beyond existing visible placeholders.
- No keyboard Up/Down list navigation.
- No Escape-to-deselect behavior.
- No route, URL, history, or persisted selected Issue state.
- No automatic selection of the first Issue.
- No breadcrumbs.
- No sticky/blurred detail header.
- No code-block copy buttons.
- No formatted/relative date display.
- No ADR for this slice.

## Further Notes

Triage label: ready-for-agent

This PRD follows the current domain model: Issue is the Beadwork data loaded from the Current Workspace; Issue List and Issue Detail are views over that Issue. The design should preserve the existing high-density dark UI and avoid unnecessary padding or decorative UI that does not help issue reading.
title: Render selected issue details in the issue explorer
